title: The Effect Of Corporate Social Responsibility (CSR) and Financial Performance On Tax Avoidance: Evidence From Energy Sector Companies Listed On The Indonesia Stock Exchange (2021-2024)
author: Putri, Kurnia
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: The study aims to analyze the effect of Corporate Social Responsibility (CSR) and financial performance on tax avoidance in energy sector companies listed on the Indonesia Stock Exchange during the period 2021-2024. This study employs a quantitative method with secondary data. The data was collected using a purposive sampling method based on specific criteria. Of the 91 companies, 18 met the requiredment for this study. The results show that CSR has a positive effect on tax avoidance, while financial performance has a negative effect on tax avoidance. It is concluded that companies engaging in tax avoidance tend to disclose more CSR information, whereas companies showing good financial performance tend to avoid from in tax avoidance practices  .

It is concluded that companies engaging in tax avoidance tend to disclose more CSR information, whereas companies showing good financial performance tend to avoid from in tax avoidance practices  .
